rationale: Traditional guidance often places heavier seating along south or west so the room remains open to north and east light.
Seating is commonly preferred so occupants face north or east during conversation and receiving guests.
A compact secondary seat follows the same traditional heavy-furniture preference as larger living-room seating.
Light central or north-east table placement is treated as acceptable when it does not block circulation.
A central rug can define the Brahmasthana-adjacent activity area while keeping furniture visually organized.
Traditional layouts keep the central zone visually calm and avoid using floor coverings to justify cluttered circulation.
Heavier storage is traditionally placed toward south, west, or south-west and kept away from north-east.
Cabinets are treated as heavy objects and are therefore preferred in south, west, or south-west zones.
Fire and warm-light objects are commonly associated with the south-east zone in popular Vastu practice.
Warm lighting colors are preferred for social rooms when the user asks for Vastu guidance.
Mirrors are commonly preferred on north or east walls and avoided in heavier south-west zones.
Plants are treated as light natural elements and commonly preferred toward north, east, or north-east.
Beds are commonly placed in the south-west of a bedroom in traditional home-layout guidance.
Sleep orientation guidance commonly avoids a north-facing head position and prefers south or east.
Wardrobes and heavy bedroom storage are traditionally preferred along south or west walls.
Bedroom cabinets are treated as heavy storage and are kept away from the north-east zone.
Traditional bedroom guidance often avoids mirrors facing the bed or occupying south-west heavy zones.
Calmer earth and light colors are commonly preferred in bedroom guidance over aggressive dark or red schemes.
Bedroom lamps can use the same warm-light south-east association while keeping glare away from sleep areas.
Bedside or reading tables are lighter objects and can sit in north, east, or north-east when practical.
Study desks are commonly oriented to support work facing east or north when the room allows.
Study seating is commonly preferred so the user faces north or east while working.
Bookcases and document storage are heavy study objects and are preferred in south or west zones.
Task lamps are light and fire-associated objects, so south-east or east placement is acceptable.
Light study colors are traditionally preferred for focus and a calmer work setting.
Popular Vastu guidance associates cooking fire with the south-east and avoids placing it in north-east.
Cooking while facing east is a common traditional recommendation when kitchen layout permits it.
Water features are commonly associated with north or north-east and kept away from the fire-oriented south-east.
Traditional grid guidance treats the center as a calm organizing area and discourages heavy obstruction.
Grounded earth and wood tones are preferred for heavy furniture when Vastu styling is requested.
